prestazioni: Replace the original Colombo-designed 1.5L V12 (Type 166, 12-cylinder, 60° bank angle, 65mm bore x 58.8mm stroke) with a fully balanced and blueprinted version featuring forged Mahle pistons (8.5:1 compression), Carrillo H-beam connecting rods, and a billet crankshaft from JP Performance to handle 12,000 RPM reliably. Pair with a custom camshaft profile by Schrick (290° duration, 12mm lift) for optimized high-RPM power delivery, retaining the original Weber 40 DCF carburetors but upgraded with velocity stacks and 45mm throttle bodies for improved airflow.

Install a bespoke dry-sump lubrication system by Peterson Fluid Systems, featuring a 10-quart oil tank, dual scavenge pumps, and a titanium oil pan with integrated baffles to prevent oil starvation during sustained lateral G-forces. Replace the stock oil cooler with a Setrab 40-row aluminum unit and integrate a secondary coolant radiator by PWR to maintain optimal operating temperatures under race conditions.

Upgrade the original 4-speed non-synchro gearbox with a Hewland 5-speed sequential transaxle (LD200), featuring a magnesium casing, straight-cut gears, and a limited-slip differential with 45% lockup. Pair with a Tilton twin-plate carbon clutch (7.25" diameter) and a custom bellhousing adapter to mate the transmission to the Type 166 V12. Replace the stock driveshafts with CV-jointed half-shafts from GKN Driveline for improved durability and reduced unsprung weight.
estetica: Fabricate a lightweight aluminum body replica of the original 1948 Scuderia Ferrari 166 F2 (chassis 002C) by Carrozzeria Fantuzzi, using 1.2mm 5052 aluminum panels hand-formed over a tubular steel subframe. Replace the stock grille with a custom mesh design by APR Performance, featuring a Ferrari Cavallino Rampante emblem milled from billet aluminum by Billet Badges Inc.

Install a period-correct Borrani wire wheel set (16" x 4.5" front, 16" x 5.5" rear) with knock-off hubs, wrapped in Pirelli Cinturato Storico tires (5.00-16 front, 6.00-16 rear) for authentic vintage aesthetics. Pair with Wilwood GP300 calipers (4-piston front, 2-piston rear) and 12.8" slotted rotors for modern braking performance while retaining the original drum brake rear axle for visual accuracy.

Apply a custom livery inspired by the 1949 Targa Florio-winning 166 F2, featuring hand-painted Rosso Corsa bodywork with Bianco Avus accents, Scuderia Ferrari shields on the doors, and a matte black hood with a functional heat extractor duct. Use PPG Vibrance Collection paints for a durable, high-gloss finish, and add period-correct decals by Studio 27, including the "166 F2" script on the rear fenders and the Ferrari prancing horse on the nose.
